informal: Let $\mathbf{x}_1, \mathbf{x}_2, \ldots$ be a sequence of the points of the product space $\prod X_\alpha$.  Show that this sequence converges to the point $\mathbf{x}$ if and only if the sequence $\pi_\alpha(\mathbf{x}_i)$ converges to $\pi_\alpha(\mathbf{x})$ for each $\alpha$.
reasoning: The informal statement says: in the product space ∏ X_α, a sequence x_n converges to x iff each projection π_α(x_n) converges to π_α(x). The Lean signature has a sequence x : ℕ → Π a, f a, a target y, and each f a carries a TopologicalSpace, which gives the product (Pi) topology automatically on Π a, f a. `Tendsto x atTop (𝓝 y)` is exactly sequence convergence, and the right-hand side says each coordinate-projection sequence j ↦ (x j) i tends to y i. The quantification over all coordinates i matches "for each α". No extra hypotheses (e.g., finiteness, Hausdorff, decidability) are added, and none are missing. This is the standard, faithful formalization.
